# BSFF — the cognitive systemic core

BSFF is not a pile of scripts; it is one self-proving core. Each layer enforces a
property the layer below cannot fake, and the top layer turns the whole stack into
a single investment decision. Every verdict in this map is generated from a machine
artifact, and `tools/build_core.py --check` fails if this page drifts from reality.

## One command proves the core

```bash
python tools/verify_all.py
```

It cascades every layer — controls, registries, grounding, conformance, the honesty
gate, the certificate chain — and ends on the derived go/no-go recommendation.

## The stack

| layer | what it proves | enforced by | live verdict |
|-------|----------------|-------------|--------------|
| **1 · Adjudication pipeline + 7 axioms** | deterministic, fail-closed verdicts | `tests/test_invariants.py` | enforced |
| **2 · Self-falsification controls** | fails AND passes correctly on ground truth | `tools/verify_controls.py` | green |
| **3 · Null + threshold registries** | no p-value without a null; no unsourced threshold | `tools/validate_{null,threshold}_registry.py` | green |
| **4 · Grounding** | every prose number re-derives from its artifact | `tools/verify_grounding.py` | green |
| **5 · Self-conformance** | every contract promise met or honestly UNVERIFIABLE | `tools/run_contract_conformance.py` | PARTIAL |
| **6 · Honesty gate** | a decorative lie cannot merge | `tools/verify_honesty.py` | green |
| **7 · Demonstration** | one command, the instrument proves itself | `tools/build_demonstration.py` | generated + synced |
| **8 · Release certificate** | the whole ordered chain certifies to one root | `tools/certify_release.py` | CERTIFIED |
| **9 · Decision gate** | does the idea earn investment? | `tools/decision_gate.py` | CONDITIONAL GO |

## Where it stands

- decision: **CONDITIONAL GO** — go to production as a falsification
  *instrument* and demarcation tool, not as a clinical/regulatory authority
  (see [`DECISION.md`](DECISION.md)).
- it does something real: a published within-subject BCI accuracy does not survive
  honest cross-subject evaluation — LOSO generalization gap **0.204**.
- it is honest about its limits: self-conformance is **PARTIAL**
  — the network/GPU/external legs are named `UNVERIFIABLE`, never faked.

## The faces of the core

- [`DECISION.md`](DECISION.md) — does the idea earn investment? (derived go/no-go)
- [`DEMONSTRATION.md`](DEMONSTRATION.md) — one-command self-proof for a reviewer
- [`CLAIM_AUDIT.md`](CLAIM_AUDIT.md) — every claim → command, value, status
- [`docs/HONESTY_AUTOMATION.md`](docs/HONESTY_AUTOMATION.md) — why it cannot lie
- [`docs/CERTIFICATE.md`](docs/CERTIFICATE.md) — the chained root over all streams

The impossible task — *prove a repository is a falsification instrument* — was made
possible by decomposing it into machine-checked properties and chaining them into
one root that fails if any link lies.
